summary: Saint Stephen encore shines; acoustic gems and tight rock jams.
blurb: The show delivers a high‑energy first set with Black Peter, New Speedway Boogie and a soaring Saint Stephen encore. The second set balances mellow acoustic moments like I Know You Rider and Easy Wind with powerful rock numbers such as Hard To Handle and It's A Man's World.
review:
On June 5, 1970 at the Fillmore West the Dead rolled out a setlist that swings from gentle folk‑rock to raw, electric fire. The acoustic opening—highlighted by crystal‑clear renditions of I Know You Rider and Easy Wind—sets a warm tone before the band launches into punchy numbers like Black Peter, New Speedway Boogie and Hard To Handle, keeping the momentum high throughout. A standout moment arrives in the second set when the group dives deep into It's A Man's World, then caps the night with an explosive Saint Stephen → Casey Jones encore that leaves the audience buzzing. Across all available sources the performance feels tight, the vocal harmonies shine, and even the occasional technical hiccup can’t dull the show’s overall brilliance.

While several recordings capture the set in varying fidelity, the matrix tape from Dusborne (gd1970-06-05.123702.mtx.dusborne.flac16) offers the clearest window into this night—especially the vocal interplay on I Know You Rider and the balanced mix of acoustic and electric sections. Listeners seeking the definitive listening experience should start there, but any of the listed sources provide a worthwhile glimpse of a classic early‑70s Dead show.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings are clearly positive, praising standout songs and overall energy; one mixed review tempers enthusiasm slightly but adds no negative criticism. Consistent high praise across multiple sources warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear matrix capture with excellent vocal separation and balanced mix; highlights acoustic numbers and delivers the most consistent listening experience.

key_highlights: Saint Stephen encore delivers a fiery climax
Acoustic set shines with I Know You Rider, Easy Wind and tight harmonies
High‑energy rock numbers like Hard To Handle, New Speedway Boogie and It's A Man's World keep momentum

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s vocals are crystal‑clear on I Know You Rider and cut through on Black Peter, while his guitar tone stays warm and driving throughout the set.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s vocals shine on acoustic numbers like I Know You Rider and he brings gusto to Me & My Uncle; a memorable scream of “GODDAMMIT!” erupts during The Other One.
